system_prompt:
你是时尚资讯主笔，负责输出一条 digest 的长文。

输入包括 digest 计划与对应的 article 摘要/引用。你的任务是生成完整中文稿件：
- title_zh、dek_zh 为中文标题与导语
- body_markdown 为简洁可读的 Markdown 正文（不要包含 JSON）
- source_article_ids 必须来自输入

规则：
- 只基于输入事实，不要编造
- 输出必须是严格 JSON，符合 schema
- 不要输出解释文本或 Markdown 代码块

输出 JSON 结构：
{
  "title_zh": "...",
  "dek_zh": "...",
  "body_markdown": "...",
  "source_article_ids": ["..."]
}

messages.content:
{"plan": {"business_date": "2026-03-29", "facet": "trend_summary", "story_keys": ["a76d4f32-201a-49fd-aa18-71d73acf0141", "c91c7aec-e4c2-492a-9c14-d3c6d69d7a26"], "article_ids": ["7cf424c7-9f0e-48ea-b0a0-28e7740ea090", "5d34d63f-cd4f-4179-b0b1-66761e143a8e"], "editorial_angle": "Willy Chavarria 借 Zara 联名系列重申 Zoot Suit 的文化抵抗精神，与此同时 Bella Hadid 以发带造型致敬 Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy，显示90年代极简美学与街头文化在当代时尚语境中的碰撞与重生", "title_zh": "从 Zoot Suit 到 CBK：复古风格背后的身份政治与极简复兴", "dek_zh": "Chavarria 将 Zoot Suit 视为'占据空间'的抵抗符号，Bella Hadid 则以 CBK 风格发带演绎90年代极简主义，两种复古趋势共同诠释时尚作为文化宣言的力量", "source_names": ["GQ", "Vogue Fashion"]}, "source_articles": [{"article_id": "7cf424c7-9f0e-48ea-b0a0-28e7740ea090", "source_name": "GQ", "title_raw": "Zara and Willy Chavarria Unveil a Fully Realized Menswear Universe", "summary_raw": "Zara's collaboration with red-hot designer Willy Chavarria just dropped. Here's what we're buying, from brawny denim jackets to zoot suit-adjacent tailoring.", "body_markdown": "# Zara and Willy Chavarria Unveil a Fully Realized Menswear Universe

Zara's collaboration with red-hot designer Willy Chavarria just dropped. Here's what we're buying, from brawny denim jackets to zoot suit-adjacent tailoring.

Over the last few years, Zara has assembled a corps d’elite of design talent to bolster its fashion-world pedigree, collaborating with longtime industry heavyweights like Stefano Pilati and behind-the-scenes savants like Aaron Levine. As of today, that buzzy Rolodex also includes Willy Chavarria, the SoCal-raised, Mexican-American designer whose vision of Chicano-influenced menswear has never looked more urgent.

VATÍSIMO, the designer’s first collaboration with the Spanish retail giant, is deeply rooted in perennial Chavarria influences—community and camaraderie are never far from his mind—including the singular style of some of the best-dressed men on the planet: his customers.

The Willy Chavarria man, the designer tells GQ, is “someone who leads with humanity. He’s strong, but also vulnerable. He understands style as a form of expression, not status.” So what better way to promote the collaboration than with a telenovela? Directed by Glen Luchford in conjunction with Chavarria himself, the high-drama short film features supermodel Christy Turlington and actor Alberto Guerra quarreling their way through a thorny love quadrangle, decked out in head-to-toe Chavarria throughout, of course.

That’s no mere figure of speech—VATÍSIMO really is a head-to-toe effort, spanning workwear-inspired denim shirts and louche trousers worthy of a vacation south of the border, but also jewelry, accessories, and at least one pair of razzle-dazzle patent leather loafers. Signature Williyisms abound, from broad-shouldered bomber jackets sliced into vests to brawny, zoot suit-adjacent tailoring—all hallmarks of Chavarria’s universe, cinematic or otherwise.

“The zoot suit has always carried a deep cultural and political weight,” Chavarria says. “It represents resistance, identity, and a kind of unapologetic presence taking up space in a world that often tries to minimize you,” Chavarria says. “Fashion moves in cycles, but for me, that makes the zoot suit language even more important. I’m drawn to volume, and to the silhouettes that feel powerful but also human.”

Unlike collaborations composed of a paltry dozen or so designs, Chavarria’s inaugural tie-up with Zara boasts nearly a hundred distinct pieces—enough for a full runway presentation. “From the beginning, it felt important to create something complete,” he says. “My work is about building a world, not just offering individual pieces. To communicate that properly, it needed to feel like a full expression of the brand.”

The full VATÍSIMO collection is available today, March 26, at Zara.com and in select Zara stores around the world.
"}, {"article_id": "5d34d63f-cd4f-4179-b0b1-66761e143a8e", "source_name": "Vogue Fashion", "title_raw": "CBK Style Claims Another Fan In Bella Hadid", "summary_raw": "Bella Hadid has hung up her cowboy hat for the time being, swapping it for a Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy-coded headband.", "body_markdown": "# CBK Style Claims Another Fan In Bella Hadid

Bella Hadid has hung up her cowboy hat for the time being, swapping it for a Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy-coded headband.

In recent years, Bella Hadid has undergone something of a style evolution, shifting from Ugg-wearing downtown New Yorker to Schiaparelli siren on the Cannes red carpet. Having taken a step back from opening or closing almost every major show on the fashion month circuit, the model and Orebella mogul then settled into a wardrobe of equestrian staples. But, with a new season comes a style shift: Hadid has hung up her horse-girl headgear for a very Carolyn Bessette Kennedy-inspired accessory.

In a recent Instagram snap offering a glimpse inside the Orebella boardroom, Hadid debuted a new look that felt worlds away from her Western-leaning aesthetic. Instead, she leaned into something a little more CBK-coded, topping off her crisp white shirt and classic trench with a slim headband. It seems, however famous you are, you can’t escape the pull of Love Story.

Unless you have been living under a rock, you’ll have noticed the CBK and JFK Jr. school of styling taking hold. (Case in point: the two men in suits and Kangol hats I saw loitering outside the office this morning.) And Hadid, already a part of the Ryan Murphy universe thanks to her cameo as a Balenciaga model in The Beauty, seems to be all in on FX-endorsed ’90s wardrobe.
"}]}
